name: Snyk
description: Developer security platform for open source (SCA), code, container and IaC scanning. Yields vulnerability and issue reports, project monitoring coverage, PR check enforcement settings and group/organisation audit logs.

steps.action: Log in to the Snyk Web UI, select the Organisation, and open the Reports tab > Issues report; set the severity filters and date range to the collection period
steps.security_note: Use a named individual or dedicated evidence-collection account, not a shared login

steps.action: Click Download CSV on the report table (or export to PDF) to capture the vulnerability and issue dataset
steps.audit_note: The CSV includes all columns shown in the UI with no row limit (5GB file cap); save with a date-stamped filename covering the report period

steps.action: Open the Projects page and capture a screenshot showing monitored projects, their import sources and last-tested timestamps as monitoring coverage evidence
steps.audit_note: Coverage evidence should show that all in-scope repositories are imported and being retested, not just that scans exist

steps.action: Navigate to Organisation Settings > Integrations, select the connected source code manager and screenshot the PR check fail conditions (e.g. fail on high or critical severity issues)
steps.audit_note: Pair this with a screenshot of SCM branch protection requiring the Snyk status check, which evidences that failing checks actually block merges

steps.action: Upload the CSV exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Check exports for internal repository names or paths that are restricted before sharing outside the security team

steps.action: In Group Settings > Notifications, set the Group-level defaults so member Organisations send issue alert emails and weekly report emails

In Organisation Settings > Notifications > Notification Settings, enable the weekly summary email and new-issue alert emails, selecting All severities or Critical and high severity to match your control threshold
steps.audit_note: The weekly report email summarises vulnerability status across all projects in the Organisation, providing a regular point-in-time evidence artefact

steps.action: Deliver the notifications to a restricted, monitored compliance mailbox and file each weekly report email into the evidence repository on receipt
steps.security_note: Limit mailbox access to authorised personnel; issue alert emails name vulnerable projects and dependencies

steps.action: Note that the Snyk UI has no scheduled CSV export — weekly report emails are summaries only. For full scheduled datasets, use the Export API (see L3). Set a recurring calendar check to catch missed weekly emails
steps.audit_note: Record any missed weekly reports and the reason; unexplained gaps weaken the evidence trail

steps.action: Create a read-only service account: navigate to Group (or Organisation) Settings > Service accounts > Create a service account, choosing a least-privilege role such as Group Viewer (or Org Collaborator / a read-only custom role)
steps.permissions_required: Group Admin (Group service account); Org Admin (Organisation service account)
steps.security_note: The token is shown only once at creation — store it immediately in a secrets manager and rotate it on a defined schedule
steps.audit_note: Service account creation is itself recorded in the Snyk audit log (group.service_account.create)

steps.action: Use the Export API for issue data: POST https://api.snyk.io/rest/groups/{group_id}/export?version=2024-10-15 (or /rest/orgs/{org_id}/export) with dataset=issues and an introduced or updated date filter; poll the returned export_id for status, then download the results
steps.permissions_required: Service account token; Enterprise plan required for API access
steps.audit_note: The POST endpoint is limited to 20 export requests per hour; status and results calls are unlimited. The pr_checks datasets from the same API evidence PR check enforcement and adoption

steps.action: Collect audit logs via GET https://api.snyk.io/rest/orgs/{org_id}/audit_logs/search?version=2024-10-15 and GET https://api.snyk.io/rest/groups/{group_id}/audit_logs/search?version=2024-10-15, paging with the cursor parameter
steps.permissions_required: Service account with Org Admin (org logs) or Group Admin (group logs) role
steps.audit_note: Audit logs cover only the past 90 days — schedule collection well inside that window (daily here) so no events age out uncollected

steps.action: Export monitoring coverage with GET https://api.snyk.io/rest/orgs/{org_id}/projects?version=2024-10-15 and reconcile the monitored project list against your source-code repository inventory
steps.permissions_required: Service account with read access to the Organisation
steps.audit_note: Coverage reconciliation evidences that scanning applies to all in-scope repositories, not merely that scans run

steps.action: Schedule the collection script daily via cron or a CI/CD pipeline, authenticating with the 'Authorization: token <API_TOKEN>' header against your regional API base URL, with retries and alerting on failed runs
steps.security_note: Read the token from the secrets manager at runtime and ensure pipeline logs never print the Authorization header

steps.action: Validate each run: confirm exports are non-empty, compare issue and project counts against the previous run's baseline, and flag anomalies for investigation
steps.audit_note: Run-over-run validation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: Snyk Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Register a webhook with POST https://api.snyk.io/v1/org/{orgId}/webhooks (url and secret in the body); Snyk sends a project_snapshot event every time a monitored project is tested, whether or not new issues are found
steps.permissions_required: Org Admin service account or user token
steps.security_note: Generate a strong random webhook secret and store it in the secrets manager alongside the receiving service

steps.action: Verify the X-Hub-Signature header (HMAC-SHA256 with the shared secret) on every delivery before processing, and pin handling to the payload version in the X-Snyk-Event header (e.g. project_snapshot/v0)
steps.security_note: Reject unsigned or mis-signed deliveries; serve the endpoint over TLS 1.2+

steps.action: Stream Snyk audit events continuously to AWS CloudTrail Lake using Snyk's event forwarding integration, giving immutable long-term retention beyond Snyk's 90-day audit log window
steps.permissions_required: Group Admin (Snyk) and CloudTrail Lake administrator (AWS)
steps.audit_note: CloudTrail Lake retention removes the 90-day collection deadline and provides tamper-evident storage for audit events

steps.action: Reconcile received project_snapshot events daily against the projects list from the REST API to compute coverage %: every monitored project should produce snapshots on its test schedule
steps.audit_note: Snapshot-versus-inventory reconciliation is the core completeness metric for this pipeline

steps.action: Run automated quality checks and a collection-health dashboard: payload schema validation, gaps in snapshot time series, duplicate deliveries, PR check adoption trends (Export API pr_checks datasets), with alerts on any gap or threshold breach
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Generate an automated quarterly collection-health report summarising coverage, gaps, webhook failures and remediation, and file it in the evidence repository
